reasoning: Wdr70 (WD repeat domain 70) functions primarily in DNA damage response and homologous recombination repair, acting as a scaffold protein that facilitates recruitment of repair factors. Its known interactions are with components of the Fanconi anemia pathway and other genome‑maintenance complexes. There is no reported role for Wdr70 in transcriptional regulation of muscle‑specific structural genes, nor any documented signaling cascade that connects Wdr70 activity to the expression of utrophin (Utrn). Consequently, knocking down Wdr70 is unlikely to have a direct or indirect effect on Utrn transcription.
